summary: Skeleton3D/PhysicalBoneSimulator does not clean up internal state when removing bones, crashes on further updates.

description.text:
# Skeleton3D/PhysicalBoneSimulator does not clean up internal state when removing bones
- Reproduced in 4.3, 4.4b3
- Windows 11, Godot-4.4beta3

## Description
There is a bug which prevents dynamically removing and then adding bones to a skeleton.

The use case for dynamically constructing skeletons is in creating generative creatures, mechanism, and so on.
For my example, I use a ball and chain simulation with dynamic number of links. The scene componentry builds a chain link up from a link template and a ball template. This repo is a test case for demonstrating the behavior.

The Chain.gd file creates a ball-and-chain simulation with a dynamic number of links that can be changed directly within the editor. It is the only script file of interest for the project.

# Project for reproducing:
[https://github.com/mikest/godot-chain/](https://github.com/mikest/godot-chain/)

## Steps to reproduce crash
1. Open project.
2. Open Scene "Chain.tscn". You should see a monkey head on a chain.
3. Select the root node.
4. In the properties panel there is a counter for the number of chain links.
5. Increase the counter by one.

## Results:
1. Editor segfaults and exits.

# Investigation
`Skeleton3D`, `PhysicalBoneSimulator3D`, and `PhysicalBone3D` all maintain a set of internal caching structures that appear to be there as speed optimizations. These caching structures are not properly kept in sync when removing physical bones or when calling `Skeleton3D::clear_bones()` to remove logical bones. In most cases, this results in numerous errors logged in the console, but on occasion, it can also result in crashes.

This PR is an attempt to reduce those errors and to reduce the conditions which trigger spurious console logging from cache size misses due to stale cache data.

To address this, I included more cache invalidation in `Skeleton3D::clear_bones()`.

I also fixed cache sync problems with the `bone_global_pose_dirty` and `nested_set_offset_to_bone_index` in `Skeleton3D::add_bone(const String &p_name)`.

Many of the logging errors that arrise after removing and adding a bone are due to caches being rebuilt before the `PhysicalBone3D` has been added to the tree. I have moved the cache rebuild to `NOTIFICATION_POST_ENTER_TREE`. This fixes the errors from `_reload_joints`.

The last change I made was to the `PhysicalBoneSimulator3D::_pose_updated()` call, which when detecting a cache size mismatch between the skeleton bone count and the internal `bones` cache will rebuild it. This happens when a physical bone is still attached and the Skeleton clears the bones and then rebuilds them to a different size. The simulator will make pose updates and cause cache misses.


## Related Issues
Fix error spam at start with Skeleton3D modifiers #102030
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102030

_NOTE: I'm not terribly familiar with the godot internals and this is my first PR, so extra attention is probably warranted._

summary: UID run/main_scene Opens when launching Editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in: v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8]
Not effected: pre-4.4, project.godot does not support UID

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

When you set a Main Scene it will always open/load this Scene in the Editor when you launch. This does not respect `open_scenes` that are saved in `.godot/editor/editor_layout.cfg`. If the Scene is closed, it should not force-open anytime the Editor is launched.

Manually editing `project.godot` to use the `res://` path to the Main Scene, will not force-open unless `open_scenes` list is empty.
(force-opening the Main Scene when the list is empty is the same behavior as 4.3)

### Steps to reproduce

Set a Main Scene (by any of the 3 methods), and close this newly set Main Scene.
Close the Editor, and open the project from the Project List window.
(Reload Current Project does not reproduce this issue)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

A blank project with two saved Scenes, first set as the Main Scene and closed, second Scene opened.

summary: Shapecast3D not detecting Area3D as collision object

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3.stable, 4.0.0.stable (official builds)

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 08 Feb 2025 18:54:55 +0000 - Wayland - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 570 Series (RADV POLARIS10) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7600K CPU @ 3.80GHz (4 Threads)

### Issue description

The Shapecast3D is not detecting Area3D, when checking for colliding objects i.e using `get_collider` or `get_collision_count` (both methods of Shapecast3D).

The description of Shapecast3D says the following
`A 3D shape that sweeps a region of space to detect CollisionObject3Ds.`

CollisionObject3D is inherited by Area3D, so it should be detected. StaticBody3D works without any problem.

### Steps to reproduce

Preconditons:
1. Shapecast3D (default settings)
2. Area3D (default settings) with CollisionShape3D.
3. Shapecast3D and CollisionShape3D needs to overlap.

Steps to reproduce (for MRP)
1. Run `main.tscn`.
2. Press "ENTER" or Spacebar key.

Actual result: A print message from `shape_cast_3d.gd` prints message `Number of colliders: 0`.
Expected result: A print message from `shape_cast_3d.gd` prints message `Number of colliders: 1`.

HINT: Leaving scene as is, but changing type from Area3D to StaticBody3D or RigidBody3D fixes the issue without any further modifications.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The minimal reproducible project contains the overlapping nodes of CollisionShape3D of Area3D and Shapecast3D. There is `shape_cast_3d.gd` that prints the number of collisions after `force_shapecast_update()`. This is not required for steps to be reproducible, I just wanted to make sure that the update of ShapeCast3D was executed.

The Shapecast3D and Area3D configuration have default settings (nothing changed).

[shapecast-area3d.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18815960/shapecast-area3d.zip)

summary: Improve `ScrollContainer::ensure_control_visible`

description.text:
Ensure minimum scrolling and maximum visibility in a single axis.

When there is only one `ScrollContainer`, it behaves the same as https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/100695#issuecomment-2645755492. Add unit tests for this case.

For the case where `ScrollContainer` nesting exists, due to the occlusion of the internal `ScrollContainer`, the control may not be visible. 

The `ScrollContainer` with **follow focus** enabled will automatically adjust its own scroll value when the control is focused. The scrolling of each level of `ScrollContainer` needs to be considered during calculation.

TODO: For the case of rotation, it may be an edge case. Since the resulting `Rect2` may be enlarged after applying the rotation transformation, the resulting intersection `Rect2` is not accurate enough. The result may not be ideal in some extreme cases.

| | In ScrollContainer space | In control space |
| :----: | :------: |  :------: | 
|Image| ![001](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/04123905-8739-487f-9d2d-315de386c1d0) |  ![002](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2e2ea3a-b4db-4a09-91be-632c5ab49bee) | 
|The intersection rect| ABFG  |A'B'C'D|
| The ideal intersection rect | CEHI | DHJI |


`ScrollContainer`'s border is red. The control's border is blue. The polygon of the intersection is DHI. The intersection rect is larger than the ideal one.

summary: Fix Embedded Game over expanded bottom panel

description.text:
- Fixes #102901

The issue came from the fact that when the bottom panel is expanded, the embedded game must be started hidden. That was not supported by Godot and the `DisplayServer`. I had to add a new `HIDDEN` flag and a new `--hidden` command line argument to start the game hidden. The new `--hidden` argument is passed to the game on startup when the embedded control is hidden (aka the bottom panel is fully expanded).

summary: Add tests for toggle mode of `Button`

description.text:
This is part of issue #43440.

Added unit tests for the toggle mode functionality of `Button`:
* `button_pressed` stay `false` if toggle mode is off.
* `button_pressed` changes by pressing if toggle mode is on.
* Disabling toggle mode resets `button_pressed` to `false`.

summary: Godot editor context and menu windows appearing only white or empty, when using Window Capture in OBS

description.text:
### Tested versions

Only tested with latest Godot Steam version: currently at this time: 4.3 stable.



### System information

Windows 11 - RTX 3060 - OBS 31 - Godot 4.3 stable

### Issue description

Out of privacy concerns, I have to specify which windows on my desktop are captured, so I figured a way utilizing Window Capture sources specifying certain executables only.

The main window of Godot is captured, but additional windows like context or menu related windows are ignored by the OBS Window Capture source; including the game window when it runs.

To fix this, I added another Window Capture that looks for the specific game window to get captured - this setup with two Window Capture sources works so far.

However, like mentioned above, any context or menu window from Godot editor, is not properly captured using an additional Window Capture source. It only shows the window fully white or empty.



### Steps to reproduce

* Add a new Window Capture source in OBS
* Select Godot editor executable and select Window 10 as capture method
* Go to the Godot editor and make a right click within the Inspector/Node panel or open a menu
* Look at OBS and observe the missing context/menu window

* Go to Godot editor and open a menu/or context window and keep it open
* Go to OBS and add another Window Capture
* Look for the other Godot executable and select it
* Toggle between capture methods (BitBit or Windows 10) and observe the full white or empty capture


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

* Add a new Window Capture source in OBS
* Select Godot editor executable and select Window 10 as capture method
* Go to the Godot editor and make a right click within the Inspector/Node panel or open a menu and keep it open
* Look at OBS and observe the missing context/menu window(s)

summary: Hide debug keystore export settings

description.text:
This PR moves the debug keystore settings under `Advanced Options`.  

These settings are only needed for users who want separate debug keystore for their projects, as there is already an option in the Editor Settings to configure the debug keystore. Also, after PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/90611, a debug keystore is automatically generated, and its path is set in the Editor Settings.

summary: Renaming target node of MultiplayerSynchronizer results in broken synchronization with wrong node being shown in replication tab

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Detected in 4.4.beta3
- can be replicated in 4.3.stable
- likely to be the same issue as [#92520](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/92520)

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot 4.4.beta3

### Issue description

If a node, which is targeted by a multiplayer synchronizer gets renamed, several things happen
- its name is not changed for the synchronization logic, as now the synchronizer will throw an error looking for the old node name
- its name is changed in the replication tab, which now shows the name of the scenes root node instead

The issue is mostly problematic, not because of the errors being thrown, but because it is really hard to spot the wrong entries inside the replication tab in larger projects

### Steps to reproduce

1. create a scene
2. add a multiplayer synchronizer and another node of any type, for this example i will name it "TestNode"

summary: Add shortcuts to reset position, rotation and scale in Spatial and Canvas Item Editor

description.text:
These changes allow resetting Node3D and Node2D transforms in the Spatial and Canvas Item Editor according to [proposal #11523](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11523).

The keybindings default to ALT+W (position), ALT+E (rotation) and ALT+R (scale) and  thus align with the default bindings to activate Move Mode (W), Rotate Mode (E) and Scale Mode (R).

Either a single selected node or multiple selected nodes can be reset and the reset can be undone.

The keybindings can be changed under Editor Settings -> Shortcuts.
![shortcuts](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4ce4083-9e27-43d7-af02-5bd13e7a678e)

- *Production edit: This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10612.*

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/04516294-a5f2-4d02-95e5-b29ae0d662dd)

This is the image after and before LightmapGI is baked. Notice how the shadow is softer in the baked image (probably not the best example). In Mobile renderer, we could preview the unbaked state by just hiding (pressing the eye icon) the LightmapGI node on the Scene dock. Only then when removing the light_data property from LightmapGI node's inspector dock will show the unbaked state.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11e3565e-5a51-4f41-9402-bf119bee86e4)

- now switch to Compatibility renderer, let it reload the project
- do the same thing, toggle hide/unhide the LightmapGI node on the inspector. Notice how it does nothing. While modifying the light_data property still does the expected result

summary: Add F2 hotkey to rename animation in SpriteFrames

description.text:
This PR adds F2 as a hotkey for "rename animation" action in animation list of Sprite Frames plugin.

Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9550

I agree with the issue; I often find myself pressing F2 by habit and failing to rename an animation. The list looks visually similar to both scene tree view and filesystem dock, and supports many similar hotkeys (create new, delete, duplicate), so it feels out of place that F2 doesn't work here.

Unlike other actions, there is no dedicated button for renaming an animation... Not sure if it's needed? Rename can already be done via double-click, and toolbar's already somewhat crowded.

### Issue description

Making a game with large outdoor scenes from birds eye view. I am trying to use compatibility rendering so there's low system requirements and I am finding conflicts with 3D settings. I have an environment in the main scene which clears the background to a custom color and uses Filmic Tonemapping. It seems that whenever I change scaling_3d_scale in my game's options panel the entire scene will permanently dim, until I reset the 3d scale back to its default 1.0 value at runtime. 1) Create a test scene
2) Add an environment that uses custom clear for the background
3) Add a toggle button that when toggled sets the scaling_3d_scale to 4.0
4) Press the toggle button and observe that changing the 3d scale causes the entire scene and background to dim
5) Press again to set back the 3d scale and observe that its fixed.

summary: Fix LineEdit only being editable via keyboard and mouse

description.text:
Currently ``LineEdit`` actions can only be submitted from Keyboard and Mouse.  This is because the event it is listening for is required to be an ``InputEventKey``.  To add gamepad support, we just need to move the ``InputEventKey`` check below the action checks.  I discovered this when I was pressing "submit" on my gamepad and the ``LineEdit`` wasn't entering edit mode.  When inspecting the source code, I noticed that it required the event be ``InputEventKey`` too early.  Here's a video of our in game keyboard.  We forked it from https://github.com/martinfuchs/Godot-Onscreen-Keyboard.  We added gamepad support in our fork here: https://github.com/Lange-Studios/Godot-Onscreen-Keyboard/tree/gamepad-support

- Reproducible in `v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8]`

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7216) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

When upgrading a project to 4.4, reflection probes remain the same size/shape.
This is a problem specifically for interior scenes, as users size probes to perfectly fit the interior of their rooms, and making a probe larger than the room worsens reflection quality.

**Converting to 4.4 causes those walls to fall into the outside _"blend"_ area of the probe, breaking reflections for walls**
_(see the gif below, the probe is sized to firmly fit the room)_
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09400f50-5c26-46b0-853b-719c9ee4d490)

I believe #99958 to be the cause, but I'm not fully sure.

**- Solutions -**
The best solution to this in my opinion would be adjusting reflection probes to make the blend area extend outside of the reflection probe's shape, instead of insetting based on the size of the reflection probe. This would also be more user-friendly in my opinion, as I wouldn't want to manually resize my reflection probes to fit my room every time I want to adjust the blend distance.
The easiest solution would be setting `blend_distance` to 0 during conversion, or calculating and resizing every reflection probe based on the default `blend_distance` of 1


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project in 4.3
2. Make a room out of CSG shapes, add reflective materials to them
3. Add a light, preferably a spotlight that does not touch the walls, as it makes it easier to see if reflections are working
4. Add a reflection probe and adjust its size to fit inside of the CSG walls
5. Convert the project to 4.4, and you will see the reflections are now broke and require manually resizing the reflection probe's green interior box to fit inside the room

summary: When using a background colour on a TextEdit, the Minimap has ugly black bars.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.3 stable, 4.2 stable, 4.1 stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable unknown - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 08 Feb 2025 18:54:55 +0000 - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) HD Graphics 620 (KBL GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7600U CPU @ 2.80GHz (4 Threads)

### Issue description

Hi. 
I'm making a sort of detective game and I'm using a TextEdit node to store notes. I am using my own custom theme to make the TextEdit look consistent with the rest of the game. I asked for help in the [godot forums](https://godotforums.org/d/41932-ugly-black-bars-when-using-minimap-in-text-edit) and even on [reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/godot/comments/1ihcbro/how_can_i_change_the_colour_of_the_lines_in_the/) but seeing as no one came for help, I assumed that it must be a bug and started to do some testing.

Without a theme, the Minimap looks okay and doesn't show these black bars. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e1f6e3b8-368c-4a6b-b5ab-0b3b6e45b23c)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c52616d5-9ab7-479b-98f4-a517d6c871da)

I noticed that when turning on the Minimap setting in the TextEdit, black bars are seen in the editor, and even in the game. I have attached a screenshot.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f204f5a-e5d7-4813-a13c-c660678f3e1c)
This isn't horrible in dark mode, just an annoyance, but since I'm also designing a white version of my theme, these black bars make the Minimap unusable.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ab8be68-b966-48ea-af8e-81cee36e489f)

I did some testing and figured out that the problem lies in the `background_color` setting of the TextEdit. If it is set to anything but `#00000000` (Even changing the alpha triggers this) the black bars appear. See the reproduction steps.

### Steps to reproduce

This bug can be seen in a blank project.
- Create a new blank project with nothing in it.
- Make a TextEdit Node
- Enable the Minimap
- Add a new theme to it, preferably a blank one
- Override the TextEdit theme 
- Go to the colours tab in the Theme
- Change the background colour
- Note how the black bars appear for every line in the Minimap if the background colour is set to anything but `#00000000`
Video attached as a demonstration in an completely empty project.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/611596db-fc24-446c-b977-cf42a5bf4a13

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Broken Text Edit Demo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18793715/Broken.Text.Edit.Demo.zip)
Not required but attached anyway. The bug can be demonstrated in any project.

summary: Fix Tree Mouse hover position

description.text:
- Fixes #102194

This PR should fixes the hovered, tooltip and click position for trees button.

The PR includes:
- A simplification of the `_determine_hovered_item` to use the `_find_button_at_pos` method which tried to do exactly the same thing, aka finding the button at a position.
- The space used by the scrollbar was not correctly calculated. At first, I tried to simply add the missing `scrollbar_h_separation` but there's some calculations in `_get_content_rect` that manage a situation where the scrollbar is inside the panel margin (when theme `scrollbar_margin_right` is >= 0). I simply used `_get_content_rect` directly like a lot of other places in the tree code. This fix was added in `_range_click_timeout`, `gui_input`, and `_find_button_at_pos`.
- The condition to check if the mouse position was hover a button was always off 2 pixels to the right because of a missing >= and a -1 needed because is a comparaison between a zero based position and a size. This fix was added in `_find_button_at_pos` and `propagate_mouse_event`.
- The margin area of the scrollbar was considered the last column and button.
- In RTL mode, the panel offset was calculated before the adjustment to `pos.x` in `_find_button_at_pos` causing an offset of a couple of pixels in RTL mode.
- Finally, I added -1 when adjusting the position in RTL mode in `_find_button_at_pos` to have the exact position. Ex: If the position is 99,0 and the size is 100,0 the actual position to use is 0,0. If the position is 0,0, the actual position to use is 99,0. I changed every place in the Tree code where these adjustments were present.


I tested in Window with the MRP from the issue and with different settings. I'm not familiar with the Tree control so I strongly suggest more test are done.

Note: There still some duplicate code between `_find_button_at_pos` and `propagate_mouse_event` but everything seems to work fine as is, I'm really not confident I can refactor this without any side effect, especially just before a new release.

summary: VoxelGI "No VoxelGI data set" warning not refreshing appropriately.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3 and 4.4beta3.

### System information

Windows 10 - Vulkan - Godot 4.3 stable and 4.4beta3 (and all the betas I bet)

### Issue description

VoxelGI "No data" warning is sticky and only seems to refresh/recalculate when the scene tree is updated, with slight differences--explained in repro steps--between 4.3 and 4.4b due to scene graph editor optimizations.

### Steps to reproduce

Repro steps for 4.3:
* Make a new VoxelGI node
* The "no data" warning appears (correctly)
* Create a new data node (doesn't matter if it's scene local or saved)
* The "no data" warning STAYS (incorrectly! we have data set!)
* Nothing you do within the node itself will make the warning go away.
* Re-arrange the node tree in any way (ie: change unrelated nodes positions in the tree) and the warning will recompute and go away.

Repro steps for 4.4b3:
* Same as above EXCEPT the warning only goes away when the VoxelGI node itself is affected. Likely due to recent optimizations in the scene graph handling (postulated in rocket chat discussion [here](https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/rendering?msg=Gb5fbB9kS1nATWWg9))

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP not included because you can do this for yourself in like 10 clicks.

summary: Exported variables in autoload scripts do not update their value in code when changed in either editor or code

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in Godot 3.4-stable, 3.6-stable, 4.0-stable, 4.3-stable, 4.4-beta1, 4.4-beta2, 4.4-beta3

bug may be older, but older versions are *complicated* to use and test... Would assume it is as old as the feature. 

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When an exported var is created in an autoloaded script, updating the variable, either in code or using the editor GUI, does not actually update it. Attempting to access the variable in a second script will return the initial value from when the scene was loaded. Attempting to access the variable in its home script will however return the updated value, as expected. 

### Steps to reproduce

In versions 3.4 ~ 3.6, open the file labeled for v3.x
In versions 4.0 ~ 4.4, open the file labeled for v4.x

The script will execute at scene open time and check the output to see results.


Basic Steps to reproduce:
- Create a scene (node type can be anything)
- Create a node ("Global" in this example), attach a script to it, make it a tool node/script, and load that script into the autoload
- Create a new node ("VarCheck" in this example), attach a script to it, and make it a tool

- Have the code below in the scripts:
- - Global:
```
@tool
extends Node

@export var example: int = 5

func _ready():
	%VarCheck.check_var()
	example = 10
	print(example)
	%VarCheck.check_var()
```
- - Check:
```
@tool
extends Node

func check_var():
	print("Example value: ", Global.example)
```

- Reload the scene and check the output
- The output will look like this:
```
Example value: 5
10
Example value: 5
```

In 4.4, using a tool_button allows the user to modify the value in the editor and then run a similar check, which will yield the same outcome. In that case, the code would look like this for Global:
```
@tool
extends Node

@export_tool_button("Check var") var checkvar = check_var
@export var example: int = 5

func check_var:
	print(example)
	%VarCheck.check_var()
```
- Using the button then changing the value in the editor sidebar GUI (for example to 10) then using it again will result in the following output:
```
5
Example value: 5
10
Example value: 5
```

summary: SpriteFrames hotkeys stop working when editing sprite_frames property of AnimatedSprite2D

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.dev [36d90c73a843afa2807a0b8dcbfbf52bdb8a759c], 4.3.stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.22631 - GLES3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 6600M (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12019.1028) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 Threads)

### Issue description

If you select AnimatedSprite2D in the editor (and presumably AnimatedSprite3D, or any node with SpriteFrames resource) and then select its SpriteFrames resource, hotkeys stop properly working in SpriteFrames editor plugin. I run into this issue very frequently when working with animated sprites.

I dug in a bit with the debugger, and apparently it's caused by an invisible zero-frame flicker of SpriteFrames plugin. Changing the animation in any way somehow causes invalidation of EditorProperty connected to the SpriteFrames resource being edited. The plugin becomes outdated (as it refers to old property instance), gets removed, but then immediately added again, and because it briefly loses visibility it also loses input focus, and all hotkeys spot working.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Load MRP
2. Select `Level` scene, then `AnimatedSprite2D` node
3. Select `Sprite Frames` property in inspector
4. By now `SpriteFrames` editor should be visible. Click on `default` animation there.
5. Notice how there is no blue border around animation list (no focus)
6. Press 'Delete', the editor prompts to delete `AnimatedSprite2D` node instead of `default` animation.

For comparison:
1. Click `Sprite Frames` property in inspector again to deselect it
2. Now, selecting `AnimatedSprite2D` and then `default` animation actually produces the focus on the Tree node, and all hotkeys work.

There is also `plugins_list.has(p_plugin)` error spam from issue #97577, not sure how related it is (repro steps are similar, also appears to be linked to plugin tracking code https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/97577#issuecomment-2657915616).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any AnimationSprite2D with assigned SpriteFrames resource will work.

[SpriteFramesHotkeyBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18791474/SpriteFramesHotkeyBug.zip)

summary: Add toggle to Game view for resetting input mode on stop

description.text:
This is a tentative change based on [a discussion](https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/editor?msg=N99zqt5p8o34BABqB) on the contributor's chat about the current behavior of the "Game" view's input mode.

Some users have reported finding the current behavior, where the selected input mode (Input/2D/3D) persists across debugging sessions, to be frustrating, as you can sometimes miss that you've left the input mode in 2D/3D when launching a new debugging session and not understand why the game doesn't respond to input.

There are several potential ways to address this, but this PR takes the approach of adding another toggle to the "triple dot" menu called "Reset Input Mode on Stop", which (like the name suggests) resets the input mode back to game input as soon as a debugging session is stopped.

This new toggle defaults to being disabled, meaning the input mode will (just like now) persist across debugging sessions by default. Its value is however persisted in the project metadata, so won't need to be enabled every editor session.

The naming and placement of all of this is of course up for discussion.

Note that the way this is implemented does not seem to apply to the "Remote Deploy" feature, like when launching the a "Runnable" export on mobile devices. I'm not sure if there's any good delineation for "stopping" a debugging session in the same way, which might perhaps mean that this needs a different approach.

summary: Fix delete Tile Source with delete key

description.text:
Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/83638

Feels like a lot of code for a small feature. 

You can now delete a selected Tile Source by pressing delete key when the Tile Sources ItemList has focus.

[Screencast_20250210_163741.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/350f39f0-dd8b-4b9c-b819-18684b49dac2)


### This however introduced a new problem. 
When you selected a tile in the tile view (to the right of the ItemList) you could use delete to remove that tile. As the action about selecting tiles did not change focus. The result now was that you deleted the source instead of the selected tile.

To fix this I made the tile view grab focus on left mouse button press (Another method could be to change focus on mouse motion instead of press?). Hopefully this does not affect any other workflow. 

This also made the delete selected tile with delete key more reliable to work, and not try to delete the TileMapLayer if TileSet editor had lost focus and you selected a new tile.

**Delete selected tile**
Before:

[Screencast_20250210_162257.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c82534e-a1bb-4ea0-816a-93bbbde7a5f7)

After:

[Screencast_20250210_162335.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0c804b55-0f84-46ef-a05a-8ea2e1709ef2)


Another small benefit of the change in focus is that the E (erase) shortcut in Setup mode now works after you have selected a tile. This did not work if there was no other control that had focus in the tile editor. 

### Scenes Collection list
Add support for delete key. As it felt strange that it wouldn't work there too.

summary: CollisionShape3D's debug_color has reduced opacity when instanced in other scenes

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8]
- Reproducible in latest master [c2732ae4b]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 SUPER (nvidia; 550.120) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

When a CollisionShape3D is the child of another node like an Area3D and this scene is then saved and instanced in another scene, the CollisionShape3D's debug_color loses opacity in this new scene. This problem does not happen if the CollisionShape3D is the original scene's root.

The following picture shows the problem. The left cube is an instance of a scene that only consists of a CollisionShape3D node. The right cube is an instance of a scene with an Area3D node as the root with the CollisionShape3D as its child. It doesn't matter what type the parent node is, any 3D node causes the problem.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a91f2ee0-aaee-4a19-9e61-a27e0747b7f0)

The bug disappears when "Editable Children" is enabled for the right cube.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/40006ba8-5336-4348-aabd-f1465cacbd97)

The bug does not occur when the entire setup is replicated with the matching 2D nodes, so I guess this behavior is not intended.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new 3D Scene
- Add a CollisionShape3D node as a child
- Add a new shape to the CollisionShape3D
- Save the scene and instance it in another empty scene
- Notice that the CollisionShape3D's debug_color is less opaque than in the original scene

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[collision_shape_3d_debug_color_bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18781569/collision_shape_3d_debug_color_bug.zip)
